name: Porro's One Pot Olympic Poultry
recipeIngredient: Chicken or Turkey parts to match servings desired.
1 quart Chicken Broth or Chicken Stock.
1 teaspoon Ground Coriander Seed (only if not using Cilantro!).
1/4 cup Cooking wine (bitter orange or other fruit is best).
1 tablespoon Crushed Red Pepper (optional substitute for Sweet Basil).
1 cup Uncooked Rice (do not use pre-cooked type; either works).
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose a large cast iron pot or, in my case, a West Bend electric frying pan and set it to simmer. Add chicken soup as desired (I often add an egg drop style).
2. While the broth is warming, wash the chicken as usual.
3. Squeeze the lemon for its juice and add without seeds.
4. Adjust the temperature until the broth just starts to boil every time the light goes out, ensuring it's below boiling point.
5. Do not add salt or sugar since there’s usually enough in the soup. The chicken should not be fully submerged; this is not intended to be a soup at the end.
6. When the simmer is fine-tuned, place poultry parts in the broth with the meaty side up. Sprinkle white and red pepper on top, cover, and let it sit for as long as desired (at least 4 hours).
7. At least 20 minutes before serving, add rice to the pot. Check if there’s enough water and adjust by bringing it back to its original level if necessary.
8. Stir well and cover again. When the rice is cooked, serve it with poultry on separate plates. I often pair this with a tossed salad made with olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic or garlic powder (see Porro's Italian Salad for details).
9. Use Cajun seasoning for added flavor, but feel free to adjust the heat as you like. Optionally add 2 teaspoons of parsley and fresh sliced mushrooms.
10. This recipe is ideal for sporting events, late guests, slow cooking without constant attention, making a one-pot, one-plate dinner with taste, easy meals when time constraints exist, and keeping well hours after adding rice.
